---
title: "FTSE Russell confirms Vietnam's upgrade to emerging market status, inclusion to begin in September"
date: "2026-04-08T01:40:13.000Z"
category: macro
impact: neutral
region: Asia
tickers: []
source: "Firstpost"
url: "https://news.google.?oc=5"
---

## Summary
FTSE Russell has officially confirmed Vietnam's upgrade to emerging market status, with inclusion set to begin in September 2026. This move will likely trigger passive fund inflows and boost Vietnam's visibility among global institutional investors.

## Key Points
• FTSE Russell officially confirms Vietnam's emerging market status upgrade
• Inclusion to begin September 2026 — passive fund inflows expected
• Vietnam joins FTSE EM index alongside Indonesia, Thailand, Malaysia, Philippines
• Benchmark impact: Vietnam expected to gain ~0.5-1% weight in broad EM indices
• Foreign ownership limits and liquidity improvements driving the upgrade
• Regional ASEAN markets may see rotational flows as Vietnam gains EM exposure

## Portfolio Impact
🟡 **IHSG / IDX**: Neutral near-term, but watch for regional fund flow rotations. Vietnam's EM inclusion may attract ASEAN-focused capital, potentially creating competition for Indonesia allocations in regional EM portfolios.

**Position Check**: No direct impact on your commodity/energy positions (ITMG, PTPS, ESSA, PGEO). Macro sentiment positive for ASEAN region overall. Monitor if passive rebalancing creates any IDX outflows in Q3 2026.
